laws.title: Public Law 2-11 | Criminal Statute of Limitations Act
laws.status_basis: Permanent general statute of limitations for Title 18 crimes; no self-repeal, sunset, or later-law repeal in the source body.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, though the source caption is internally mismatched.
provisions.evidence: "This piece of legislation shall be referredto as the \"City Level Infrastructure Act of 2021\"."

provisions.text_summary: Immediate effective date upon passage.
provisions.evidence: "This Act shall go into effectimmediately upon passage."

provisions.text_summary: Conflict clause voiding inconsistent laws.
provisions.evidence: "Any part of any act,joint resolution, or codified law that conflicts with this Actshall be considered null and void."

provisions.text_summary: Severability clause.
provisions.evidence: "this act shall be severable"

provisions.text_summary: Creates a 75-day limitations period for Title 18 crimes, with 150 days for listed chapters, and bars prosecution after expiration.
provisions.exact_change: Add a new Title 18 statute-of-limitations provision: 75 days for Title 18 offenses except listed chapters 29, 37, 47, 111A, 113B, 115, and 118; 150 days for those listed chapters; accrual at the time of the offense.
provisions.evidence: "Any Crime under Title 18 shall have a statute of limitationsof limitations of 75 days"; "All criminal prosecutions tried under clauses (1) - (7)"; "The Statute of Limitations for an offense shall commenceat the time the offense takes place."

current_implementation.detail: The draft note at Title 18 section 1 carries a full-text dump and Trello link, but the source creates a real statute-of-limitations rule that should be enacted as operative Title 18 text.

recommended_actions: Codify the Title 18 statute-of-limitations rule as operative text.
Remove the full-text dump and Trello link from rp-pl002011-codification.
Correct the law status to current.

laws.title: Public Law 2-12 | Fee Prohibition Act
laws.status_basis: Permanent federal court-fee prohibition with no self-repeal, sunset, or later-law repeal in the source body.

provisions.text_summary: Short title.
provisions.evidence: "This piece of legislation shall be referredto as the \"City Level Infrastructure Act of 2021\"."

provisions.text_summary: Immediate effective date.
provisions.evidence: "This Act shall go into effectimmediately upon passage."

provisions.text_summary: Conflict clause.
provisions.evidence: "Any part of any act,joint resolution, or codified law that conflicts with this Actshall be considered null and void."

provisions.text_summary: Severability clause.
provisions.evidence: "this act shall be severable"

provisions.text_summary: Prohibits court fees in United States courts except as criminal punishment.
provisions.exact_change: Add a new Title 28 rule barring courts, including the Supreme Court, from imposing or collecting fees in proceedings unless the charge is imposed as punishment for a criminal offense.
provisions.evidence: "No Court in the United States, including the SupremeCourt, shall impose, collect, or otherwise require fees inany of its proceedings unless imposed as a punishment for acriminal offense."

current_implementation.detail: The XML places this substantive court-fee rule as a note at Title 50 section 1 and marks it repealed/superseded without source support; the rule belongs in Title 28 as operative text.

recommended_actions: Codify the fee prohibition as operative Title 28 text.
Remove the full-text dump and Trello link from rp-pl002012-codification.
Correct the law status to current and move it off Title 50.

laws.title: Public Law 2-13 | The Employees Termination Notification Act of 2021
laws.status_basis: Permanent employment-notice requirement with no self-repeal, sunset, or later-law repeal in the source body.

provisions.text_summary: Short title.
provisions.evidence: "This Act shall be referred to as \"The Employees Termination Notification Act of2021\"."

provisions.text_summary: Severability clause and no-newer-law override language.
provisions.evidence: "This Act shall be severable"; "shall not be superseded by any newer law unless that law specifically declares its intent to supersede this Act's provisions."

provisions.text_summary: Immediate effective date upon passage and signature.
provisions.evidence: "This bill shall take effect immediately upon passage of both chambersof Congress and signed by the President."

provisions.text_summary: Requires notice of termination reasons and delivery within 48 hours for federal-agency and private-business employees.
provisions.exact_change: Add a new employment-notice provision requiring a terminating employer to give the employee the reason for termination within 48 hours, including federal-agency and private-business contexts.
provisions.evidence: "A High Rank employee from any Federal Agency and Private Businessshall notify an employee of his/her termination with the reason of theirtermination."; "has forty-eight hours (48 hours) to present the employee witha reason for termination."
provisions.notes: Federal-agency implementation may warrant a Title 5 cross-reference.

current_implementation.detail: The XML parks a substantive employment-notice rule as a note in Title 5 section 101; the law should be enacted as real labor text, with a Title 5 cross-reference for federal agencies if needed.

recommended_actions: Codify the termination-notice rule as operative Title 29 text.
Add a Title 5 cross-reference for federal-agency employees if the editor wants a federal-workforce placement.
Remove the Trello link and correct the repealed/superseded label.

laws.title: Public Law 2-14 | National Federal Torts Act
laws.status_basis: Permanent federal civil-torts framework and jurisdictional rules; no self-repeal, sunset, or later-law repeal in the source body.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, immediate effective date, conflict clause, and severability.
provisions.evidence: "National Federal Torts Act"; "immediately upon passage"; "conflicts with this Act shall be considered null and void"; "This act shall be severable"

provisions.text_summary: Creates federal civil-suit jurisdiction for recognized tort claims and limits review of disputes outside recognized U.S. territory/districts.
provisions.exact_change: Add a new civil-jurisdiction provision for District Courts covering recognized civil tort claims and the territorial limitation language in the source.
provisions.evidence: "Any person who is a Citizen of the United States ofAmerica shall have the ability to launch a civil lawsuit inthe United States District Court"; "No Court of the United States shall have the jurisdictionto review civil disputes that occur outside of arecognized territory or district"

provisions.text_summary: Defines the individual civil torts: trespassing, battery, assault, false imprisonment, negligence, negligence per se, breach of contract, fraud, wrongful death, tortious interference, civil conspiracy, abuse of process, trover, harassment, and defamation.
provisions.exact_change: Codify the enumerated tort causes of action and damages caps as operative Title 28 civil-law text.
provisions.evidence: "TRESPASSING"; "BATTERY"; "ASSAULT"; "FALSE IMPRISONMENT"; "NEGLIGENCE"; "BREACH OF CONTRACT"; "FRAUD"; "WRONGFUL DEATH"; "TORTIOUS INTERFERENCE"; "CIVIL CONSPIRACY"; "ABUSE OF PROCESS"; "TROVER"; "HARASSMENT"; "DEFAMATION"

provisions.text_summary: Sets the government-liability rules: no injunctive relief of apologies/repentance, no monetary relief, official-capacity treatment, defense/settlement authority, intervention, and qualified-immunity override if the government refuses to defend.
provisions.exact_change: Codify the government-liability and defense rules for suits against the United States or agents acting in official capacity.
provisions.evidence: "The Government shall be immune from all offenses other than those listed in this section"; "No monetary relief shall be rewarded"; "Qualified immunity shall not be a valid defense"

provisions.text_summary: Creates false-arrest relief and expungement rules.
provisions.evidence: "FALSE ARREST - Any Law Enforcement Officer"; "shall be subject to injunctive relief inexpungement of the arrest."

provisions.text_summary: Creates a civil action for systematic rights violations with permanent restraining-order relief.
provisions.exact_change: Codify the systemic-rights violation cause of action and injunctive remedy.
provisions.evidence: "CIVIL ACTION FOR SYSTEMATIC RIGHTS VIOLATION"; "shall be liable to injunctive relief by a permanent restraining order against the government."

current_implementation.detail: The draft note at Title 28 section 1 preserves none of the required tort, jurisdiction, or government-liability text; it is only a placeholder note plus dump/Trello baggage.

recommended_actions: Codify the jurisdiction, tort, and government-liability rules as operative Title 28 text.
Remove the full-text dump and Trello link from rp-pl002014-codification.
Correct the law status to current.

laws.title: Public Law 3-15 | Admission to the United Nations Act
laws.status_basis: Permanent notification duty to the Archivist; no self-repeal, sunset, or later-law repeal in the source body.

provisions.text_summary: Short title.
provisions.evidence: "Admission to United Nations Act"

provisions.text_summary: Effective date.
provisions.evidence: "This Act shall go into effect immediately upon passage."

provisions.text_summary: Conflict clause.
provisions.evidence: "Any part of any act, jointresolution, or codified law that conflicts with this Act shall beconsidered null and void."

provisions.text_summary: Severability clause.
provisions.evidence: "This act shall be severable"

provisions.text_summary: Requires the President to notify the Archivist of all bills signed.
provisions.exact_change: Add a Title 44 reporting duty requiring the President to notify the Archivist of the United States of all bills signed.
provisions.evidence: "the President of the United States, upon theenactment of this Act, shall beobligated to notify the Archivist of the United States of all Bills he so signs."

current_implementation.detail: The law is a Title 44 records-duty rule, but the XML only has a note at Title 22 section 2651 with a full-text dump and Trello link.

recommended_actions: Codify the Archivist-notification duty as operative Title 44 text.
Remove the full-text dump and Trello link from rp-pl003015-codification.
Correct the law status to current.

laws.title: Public Law 3-16 | The Capitol Police Establishment
laws.status_basis: Creates the Capitol Police Board, protective powers, arrest authority, Capitol-complex policing duties, and outside-jurisdiction deployment rules; no self-repeal or sunset.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, reasoning, and effective date.
provisions.evidence: "The CPE Act of 2021"; "shall be severable"; "Due to the uprising of Congressional Members needing protection"; "take effect immediately"

provisions.text_summary: Establishes the Capitol Police Board and its membership and authorities.
provisions.exact_change: Create the Capitol Police Board with the listed congressional officers and grant appointment/removal/regulatory authority.
provisions.evidence: "The following shall be members of the Capitol Police Board"; "Appoint a Capitol Police Chief"; "Remove a sitting Capitol Police Chief"; "The power to regulate the security of the Capitol Buildings"

provisions.text_summary: Authorizes protection of Members of Congress and families, warrantless arrests in protective duties, use of Capitol Police property/equipment, and penalties for obstruction.
provisions.exact_change: Codify the protective-service authority, arrest authority, and obstruction penalty provisions.
provisions.evidence: "the United States CapitolPolice is authorized to protect"; "to make arrests withoutwarrant"; "shall be fined not more than $300 orimprisoned not more than one year"

provisions.text_summary: Makes Capitol Police the principal agency for policing the Capitol complex and covered office buildings.
provisions.exact_change: Codify Capitol-complex policing authority and the covered-building definitions.
provisions.evidence: "The Capitol Police shall police the United States Capitol Buildings and Grounds"; "shall be the principal agency tasked with the policing of thecapitol complex"

provisions.text_summary: Restricts deployment outside jurisdiction absent board notice/approval, with emergency, intelligence, and protective-service exceptions.
provisions.exact_change: Codify the outside-jurisdiction deployment approval rule and exceptions.
provisions.evidence: "The Chief of the Capitol Police may not deploy any officer outside of theareas established by law"; "suchboard approves of the deployment"; "Responding to an imminent threat or emergency"

current_implementation.detail: The XML note is parked in Title 18 section 1, but the source creates Title 2 legislative-branch police text; no operative text was added.

recommended_actions: Codify the Capitol Police framework as operative Title 2 text.
Remove the full-text dump and Trello link from rp-pl003016-codification.
Correct the law status to current.

laws.title: Public Law 3-17 | The Diplomatic Protections Act
laws.status_basis: Permanent diplomatic-protection and immunity rules; no self-repeal, sunset, or later-law repeal in the source body.

provisions.text_summary: Short title.
provisions.evidence: "Diplomatic Protections Act"

provisions.text_summary: Effective date.
provisions.evidence: "This Act shall go into effect immediatelyupon passage"

provisions.text_summary: Conflict clause.
provisions.evidence: "Any part of any act, jointresolution, or codified law that conflicts with this Act shall beconsidered null and void."

provisions.text_summary: Severability clause.
provisions.evidence: "This act shall be severable"

provisions.text_summary: Defines foreign diplomats, requires State Department protection, provides for removal and deportation, grants diplomatic immunity on American soil when authorized, and gives a 24-hour departure timetable.
provisions.exact_change: Codify the diplomatic-protection, deportation, immunity, and departure-timetable rules in Title 22.
provisions.evidence: "Foreign Diplomat"; "provide adequate protection"; "to be deported to their country of origin"; "Diplomatic Immunity"; "24hr timetable to leave the United States"

recommended_actions: Codify the diplomatic-protection rules as operative Title 22 text.
Remove the full-text dump and Trello link from rp-pl003017-codification.

laws.title: Public Law 3-18 | Amendment to S.12
laws.status_basis: Express amendment to PL 3-16 Section V; no self-repeal, sunset, or later-law repeal in the source body.

provisions.text_summary: Short title.
provisions.evidence: "Amendment S.12"

provisions.text_summary: Severability clause.
provisions.evidence: "This Act shall be severable"

provisions.text_summary: Reasoning clause identifying the amendment target.
provisions.evidence: "This is an amendment to the Capitol Police Establishment S.12"

provisions.text_summary: Disbands Section V of S.12 and replaces it with 'Capitol Police Outside Prescribed Jurisdiction' powers, including D.C. law enforcement, emergency response, intelligence gathering, and protective services.
provisions.exact_change: Replace Section V of PL 3-16 in full with the new outside-jurisdiction authority and renamed section title.
provisions.evidence: "Section V and its entirely shall be disbanded"; "Capitol Police Outside Prescribed Jurisdiction"; "Capitol Police Shall be authorized to enforce all federal andmunicipal laws of Washington D.C"

current_implementation.detail: The draft note at Title 50 section 1 does not amend PL 3-16's text; the amendment must be integrated into the underlying Capitol Police statute.

recommended_actions: Amend PL 3-16 Section V with the replacement text from this act.
Remove the Trello link and keep only a concise history note if desired.
Correct the law status to current.
